response:
The first phase needs ten man-hours to start.
Phase two requires twenty man-hours for work.
Phase three takes forty man-hours to finish.
Phase four uses eighty man-hours of time.
Phase five needs one hundred sixty man-hours.
These values sum to three hundred ten total.
Each step doubles the effort needed before.
A geometric pattern guides this whole plan.
Now delays add fifty percent more time.
Weeks double in count for each phase too.
Initial twenty weeks stretch out by half.
New duration becomes thirty weeks in all.
No order changes as teams keep moving.
Fortune favors those who adapt to change.
The project still moves toward its goal.
